step_by_step_reasoning: First, we identify and map all 8 entities from the World Model to our JSON node list, using the exact names and service types. Next, we construct the connections based on the visual_connections list. We map the event-driven healing sequence: EC2 App generates infrastructure events to CW Events, which then triggers SNS. SNS notifies external channels (Mail/ITSM) and triggers the healing Lambda function. The Lambda function coordinates recovery actions via Systems Manager (SSM) and performs healing on the EC2 Application instance. Finally, we model CloudWatch continuously monitoring the EC2 App.

graph.name: Linke SAP Auto-Healing Architecture
graph.notes: An auto-healing framework developed by Linke for SAP environments on AWS. It leverages serverless monitoring via CloudWatch and CloudWatch Events to automatically detect application or infrastructure level failures, triggering an automated Lambda-driven recovery workflow utilizing AWS Systems Manager (SSM).

nodes.name: EC2 App
nodes.notes: Hosts the SAP application server instance.

nodes.name: SAP DB
nodes.notes: Database layer of the SAP system (such as SAP HANA or other supported DBs).

nodes.name: CLOUDWATCH
nodes.notes: Monitors the performance and health metrics of the SAP application.

nodes.name: CW EVENTS
nodes.notes: Captures infrastructure state changes and triggers notifications or remediation.

nodes.name: SNS
nodes.notes: Fans out alerts and healing requests to notification services and automated Lambda routines.

nodes.name: Mail / ITSM
nodes.notes: Notification channels such as email or IT Service Management platforms.

nodes.name: LAMBDA
nodes.notes: A serverless healing function triggered to recover the failed SAP application elements.

nodes.name: SSM
nodes.notes: Performs automated Run Command actions directly inside the guest operating system.

edges.notes: CloudWatch monitors the EC2 Application instance for performance issues and service availability.
Infrastructure events are emitted from the EC2 instance to CloudWatch Events upon failure or critical status changes.
CloudWatch Events routes the failure event to an SNS topic.
SNS notifies operators and logs issues in the Mail/ITSM ticketing systems.
SNS triggers the designated healing Lambda function.
The healing Lambda invokes Systems Manager (SSM) to run commands on the affected server.
The Lambda recovery flow initiates programmatic restoration actions directly affecting the EC2 instance.
